bank_title: Math Expression for Simple Harmonic Motion
title: Spring-Mass Position Function (Equilibrium Start)
question:
A block of mass $m$ is attached to an ideal spring with spring constant $k$ and is placed on a horizontal, frictionless surface. The block undergoes simple harmonic motion with an amplitude $A$.

At time $t=0$, the block is passing through the equilibrium position ($x=0$) with a velocity $v$ in the positive $x$-direction.

Which of the following expressions correctly describes the position $x(t)$ of the block as a function of time?
